author: Ruohang Feng (rh@vonng.com)
description: Kafka KRaft cluster overview: identity, member inventory, reliability, throughput, consumer backlog, alerts, exporters and logs for one cluster selected by cls. Entry point of the Kafka monitoring hierarchy: drill down to Kafka Instance / Topic / Consumer.

panels.description: Identity strip: the Kafka cluster name (`cls` label). All panels on this page are scoped to this cluster; the KAFKA dropdown navigates to sibling dashboards with the same scope.

Member inventory of this cluster, one row per Kafka process (JMX target): scrape liveness (Alive), live KRaft quorum state (Raft: blue leader / green follower), static role, partition replicas hosted and partitions led (`kafka_server_replica_manager_*`), and JVM heap utilization (`kafka:ins:jvm_heap_used_ratio`, amber ≥75%, red ≥90%). Click Instance to open Kafka Instance, Node IP to open Node Instance.
panels.title: Kafka Instances

panels.description:
Cluster key state, one horizontal strip per fact:

- **Controller**: node id of the active KRaft leader (`kafka_server_raft_current_leader`).
- **Raft Epoch**: current quorum epoch (`kafka_server_raft_current_epoch`); it increments on every controller election, so a climbing epoch means election churn.
- **Metadata HW**: metadata log high watermark (`kafka_server_raft_high_watermark`), the committed offset of the cluster metadata log.
- **JMX Up / Exporter Up**: live scrape targets — Kafka processes (`up{role=~".+"}`) and kafka_exporter replicas (`up{role=""}`).
- **Health**: composite verdict, green only when: exporter reachable, brokers registered, every JMX target up, exactly one active controller, zero offline / under-min-ISR / under-replicated / leaderless partitions.

targets.expr: max by (cls) (up{job="$job",role="",cls="$cls"}) * on (cls) ((max by (cls) (kafka_brokers{job="$job",cls="$cls"}) or on (cls) (0 * max by (cls) (up{job="$job",role="",cls="$cls"}))) > bool 0) * on (cls) ((sum by (cls) (max without (ins, ip, instance) (kafka_topic_partition_under_replicated_partition{job="$job",cls="$cls"})) or on (cls) (0 * max by (cls) (up{job="$job",role="",cls="$cls"}))) == bool 0) * on (cls) ((sum by (cls) (max without (ins, ip, instance) (kafka_topic_partition_leader{job="$job",cls="$cls"}) < bool 0) or on (cls) (0 * max by (cls) (up{job="$job",role="",cls="$cls"}))) == bool 0) * on (cls) (min by (cls) (up{job="$job",role=~".+",cls="$cls"})) * on (cls) ((sum by (cls) (kafka_controller_active_controller_count{job="$job",cls="$cls"}) or on (cls) (0 * min by (cls) (up{job="$job",role=~".+",cls="$cls"}))) == bool 1) * on (cls) ((max by (cls) (kafka_controller_offline_partition_count{job="$job",cls="$cls"}) or on (cls) (0 * min by (cls) (up{job="$job",role=~".+",cls="$cls"}))) == bool 0) * on (cls) ((sum by (cls) (kafka_server_replica_manager_under_min_isr_partitions{job="$job",cls="$cls"}) or on (cls) (0 * min by (cls) (up{job="$job",role=~".+",cls="$cls"}))) == bool 0)

panels.description: Registered brokers reported by kafka_exporter (`kafka_brokers`, deduplicated across redundant exporters). Red when zero: either the whole cluster is down or no exporter can reach it.
Distinct topics on this cluster, including internal ones such as __consumer_offsets (`count of kafka_topic_partitions by topic`).

targets.expr: count(count by (cls, topic) (kafka_topic_partitions{job="$job",cls="$cls",topic=~"$topic"})) or on() vector(0)

panels.description: Total partition count across all topics (`sum of kafka_topic_partitions`). This is partitions, not replicas: replication factor multiplies storage, not this number.

targets.expr: sum(max by (cls, topic) (kafka_topic_partitions{job="$job",cls="$cls",topic=~"$topic"})) or on() vector(0)

panels.description: Consumer groups with committed offsets (`kafka_consumergroup_current_offset` grouped by consumergroup). Zero simply means nothing consumes via group offsets. Zero simply means nothing consumes via group offsets — a quiet state, not a failure.

targets.expr: count(count by (cls, consumergroup) (kafka_consumergroup_current_offset{job="$job",cls="$cls",topic=~"$topic",consumergroup=~"$group"})) or on() vector(0)

panels.description: Partitions without an elected leader (`kafka_topic_partition_leader < 0`). Any nonzero value means unavailable partitions: producers and consumers are blocked. Red at ≥1.

targets.expr: sum(max without (ins, ip, instance) (kafka_topic_partition_leader{job="$job",cls="$cls",topic=~"$topic"}) < bool 0) or on() vector(0)

panels.description: Partitions with fewer in-sync replicas than replicas (`kafka_topic_partition_under_replicated_partition`). Durability margin is reduced; sustained values page via KafkaUnderReplicatedPartitions. Red at ≥1.

targets.expr: sum(max without (ins, ip, instance) (kafka_topic_partition_under_replicated_partition{job="$job",cls="$cls",topic=~"$topic"})) or on() vector(0)

panels.description: Total unconsumed records across every consumer group and topic (`kafka:csg_topic:lag` summed — the same recording rule the KafkaConsumerLagGrowing alert evaluates). Amber ≥10k, orange ≥100k, red ≥1M.
Currently firing kafka-category alerts on this cluster (`ALERTS{alertstate="firing",category="kafka"}`). Red at ≥1; see the alert panel below for names and timing.
Broker-authoritative throughput from JMX: messages in per second (left) and replication-exclusive client bytes in/out (right axis).
panels.title: Cluster Throughput

panels.description: Alert timeline for this cluster in the standard Pigsty style: solid bands are firing alerts (`ALERTS{alertstate="firing"}`), pale bands are pending ones still inside their for-window. Every alert with cls of this cluster is shown — kafka, node and infra categories alike. Click a legend entry to open it in Alertmanager.

Replication safety of the cluster over time: under-replicated partitions (`kafka_topic_partition_under_replicated_partition` summed, deduplicated) and leaderless partitions (`kafka_topic_partition_leader < 0`). Both belong at zero: URP means durability margin lost, leaderless means the partition is unavailable to clients.

targets.expr: sum(clamp_min(max without (ins, ip, instance) (kafka_topic_partition_replicas{job="$job",cls="$cls",topic=~"$topic"}) - max without (ins, ip, instance) (kafka_topic_partition_in_sync_replica{job="$job",cls="$cls",topic=~"$topic"}), 0))

panels.title: Partition Reliability

panels.description: Control-plane stability: KRaft controller elections (`rate(kafka_controller_elections_total)`), broker heartbeat timeouts and fenced brokers (`kafka_controller_timed_out_broker_heartbeats_total`, `kafka_controller_fenced_broker_count`), unclean leader elections (data loss risk!), and cluster-wide ISR shrink/expand rates. All of these belong at zero in steady state — any activity here explains leadership and availability turbulence seen elsewhere.
panels.title: Controller & ISR Churn

panels.description: Partition leaders per broker (`kafka_topic_partition_leader` value-counted by broker id). Roughly equal lines mean balanced client load; one broker accumulating leaders after failovers is a hotspot until preferred-leader election restores placement (see NonPreferred in Topic Inventory).

targets.expr: count_values by (cls) ("broker", max without (ins, ip, instance) (kafka_topic_partition_leader{job="$job",cls="$cls",topic=~"$topic"}) >= 0)

panels.title: Leader Distribution

panels.description: Observed log-end offset growth and consumer committed-offset progress. Commit progress is not broker network throughput.
panels.title: Append / Commit Rate

panels.description: Leader append rate per broker (`kafka:ins:messages_in_rate5m`, JMX-authoritative). Uneven lines reveal leader/producer skew: the busiest broker bounds cluster headroom even when the average looks comfortable. Compare with the Leaders column in the member table above.
panels.title: Broker Messages In

panels.description: Total backlog per consumer group: `kafka:csg_topic:lag` (deduplicated, per-topic recording rule) summed by group, topk $topk. This is the exact series the KafkaConsumerLagGrowing alert evaluates (>100k records and still growing after 30m).
panels.title: Consumer Lag by Group
Network I/O
panels.description: Cluster-wide bytes per second, split by purpose: client traffic (`kafka:ins:bytes_in/out_rate5m` summed) vs inter-broker replication (`kafka_server_broker_replication_bytes_in/out_total` rates summed). Replication roughly tracks client-in × (RF−1); a replication surge without client growth means followers are catching up after an outage or reassignment.

panels.title: Request Rate
panels.description: Cluster-wide API request throughput by request type (`rate(kafka_network_request_total)` summed by request). The mix characterizes the workload: Produce/Fetch dominate data flow, Metadata/Heartbeat/ApiVersions dominate idle or chatty-client clusters. Sudden Metadata storms usually mean clients are rediscovering leadership after a failover.

panels.title: Request Errors
panels.description: Cluster-wide client-visible failures: failed produce / fetch request rates (`kafka_server_broker_failed_produce/fetch_requests_total`) plus the error-code breakdown (`kafka_network_request_errors_total{error!="NONE"}` summed by error). The error code names the cause directly — NOT_ENOUGH_REPLICAS is under-min-ISR, NOT_LEADER_OR_FOLLOWER accompanies leadership moves, REQUEST_TIMED_OUT indicates saturation.

panels.title: JVM Heap Usage
panels.description: JVM heap utilization per broker (`kafka:ins:jvm_heap_used_ratio` = heap used / heap max). Sustained values above 75–90% mean GC pressure and request latency before OOM; one broker diverging from its peers usually maps to skewed partition leadership. The KafkaJvmHeapHigh alert fires above 90% for 15m.

panels.title: Broker CPU Usage
panels.description: Kafka JVM CPU consumption per broker in cores (`kafka:ins:jvm_cpu_cores`). Uneven lines expose hot brokers; compare with Broker Messages In and the Leaders column above to attribute the skew to leadership placement or to a heavy client.

panels.title: GC Pressure
panels.description: Garbage-collection wall-time share per broker (`rate(jvm_gc_collection_seconds_sum)` summed over collectors — seconds of GC per second). Above ~0.05 the broker spends 5% of its time collecting and request latency jitter follows; correlate spikes with heap usage on the left.
Topic partitioning, replication, leadership, offset span, and append progress. RF is the maximum replica count; MinISR is the minimum observed in-sync replica count.

targets.expr: label_join(max by (cls, topic) (max without (ins, ip, instance) (kafka_topic_partition_replicas{job="$job",cls="$cls",topic=~"$topic"})), "key", " / ", "cls", "topic")
label_join(min by (cls, topic) (max without (ins, ip, instance) (kafka_topic_partition_in_sync_replica{job="$job",cls="$cls",topic=~"$topic"})), "key", " / ", "cls", "topic")
label_join(sum by (cls, topic) (max without (ins, ip, instance) (kafka_topic_partition_under_replicated_partition{job="$job",cls="$cls",topic=~"$topic"})), "key", " / ", "cls", "topic")
label_join(sum by (cls, topic) (1 - max without (ins, ip, instance) (kafka_topic_partition_leader_is_preferred{job="$job",cls="$cls",topic=~"$topic"})), "key", " / ", "cls", "topic")
label_join(sum by (cls, topic) (max without (ins, ip, instance) (kafka_topic_partition_current_offset{job="$job",cls="$cls",topic=~"$topic"})), "key", " / ", "cls", "topic")
label_join(sum by (cls, topic) (max without (ins, ip, instance) (kafka_topic_partition_oldest_offset{job="$job",cls="$cls",topic=~"$topic"})), "key", " / ", "cls", "topic")

label_join(sum by (cls, topic) (clamp_min(max without (ins, ip, instance) (kafka_topic_partition_current_offset{job="$job",cls="$cls",topic=~"$topic"}) - max without (ins, ip, instance) (kafka_topic_partition_oldest_offset{job="$job",cls="$cls",topic=~"$topic"}), 0)), "key", " / ", "cls", "topic")

panels.title: Topic Inventory

panels.description: Consumer groups on this cluster with member count, consumed topics, total backlog and commit rate. Click a group to open the Kafka Consumer dashboard.

targets.expr: count by (consumergroup) (count by (consumergroup, topic) (max without (ins, ip, instance) (kafka_consumergroup_current_offset{job="$job",cls="$cls",consumergroup=~"$group"})))

panels.title: Consumer Groups

panels.description: kafka_exporter replica liveness (`up{job="kafka",role=""}` per replica): teal while the replica answers scrapes, red when down. Two replicas scrape the same cluster for redundancy and their logical series are deduplicated in every query, so losing one replica does not lose data — losing both blinds topic/partition/consumer panels while brokers keep serving.
panels.title: Exporter Availability

panels.description: kafka_exporter process load: CPU usage (`rate(process_cpu_seconds_total{role=""})`, share of one core) and goroutines. Cost scales with topic/partition/group cardinality; goroutine spikes usually mean a slow broker is blocking metadata collection.
panels.title: Exporter Runtime

panels.description: kafka_exporter runtime cost: resident memory (`process_resident_memory_bytes`), Go heap in use, scrape duration (right axis, `scrape_duration_seconds`) and samples per scrape. Rising duration tracks growing cardinality; hitting the scrape timeout surfaces as KafkaExporterDown flaps.
panels.title: Exporter Memory / Scrape Cost

panels.description: Kafka server log lines per level over time (LogsQL `stats by (level) count()`). A surge of warn/error lines usually precedes metric-visible failure and pinpoints its start.

targets.expr: job:syslog ip:in(${members:doublequote}) (unit:kafka OR app:kafka) | stats by (level) count() as count

panels.title: Kafka Log Volume

panels.description: kafka_exporter log lines per level over time. Bursts correlate with broker unavailability (connection refused) or metadata timeouts.

targets.expr: job:syslog ip:in(${members:doublequote}) (unit:kafka_exporter OR app:kafka_exporter) | stats by (level) count() as count

panels.title: Exporter Log Volume

panels.description: Raw Kafka server logs from VictoriaLogs: LogsQL `job:syslog (unit:kafka OR app:kafka)` filtered to the member IPs of this scope (log streams carry node identity, so filtering is by ip, not by kafka cls). Fields: level, ip, unit, message.

targets.expr: job:syslog ip:in(${members:doublequote}) (unit:kafka OR app:kafka) | fields level,ip,unit,_msg

panels.title: Kafka Service Logs

panels.description: Raw kafka_exporter logs from VictoriaLogs (`unit:kafka_exporter`), filtered to member IPs. Connection errors here explain gaps in topic/consumer panels while broker JMX metrics continue.

targets.expr: job:syslog ip:in(${members:doublequote}) (unit:kafka_exporter OR app:kafka_exporter) | fields level,ip,unit,_msg

panels.title: Kafka Exporter Logs

title: Kafka Overview
